2009-11-01.title: Corrections
2009-11-01.abstract: An article last Sunday about an academic reassessment that challenges what is known about the 1415 Battle of Agincourt in France, where an exhausted English army defeated heavily armed French nobles, gave an outdated name for the academic institution where Kelly DeVries, an authority on medieval warfare who was quoted, is a professor of history. It is Loyola University Maryland; until Sept. 25, it had been “Loyola College in Maryland.”.

2009-11-01.title: Corrections
2009-11-01.abstract: Because of an editing error, an article last Sunday about demonstrations that call for a reduction in carbon dioxide levels in the atmosphere to 350 parts per million, which some prominent scientists consider the limit to avoid disruptions from global warming, misstated the job at the Environmental Defense Fund formerly held by Michael Oppenheimer, a Princeton University scientist, who said it would be a herculean accomplishment to hold those levels below 450 parts per million. It was chief scientist, not scientist.

2009-11-01.title: Vettel Drives to Abu Dhabi Victory and Second in the Series
2009-11-01.abstract: Sebastian Vettel won the inaugural Abu Dhabi Grand Prix.

2009-11-01.title: Agnes’s Paddock Notes From Abu Dhabi
2009-11-01.abstract: Paddock notes from the inaugural Abu Dhabi Grand Prix.

2009-11-01.title: Scattering of Attacks in Iraq
2009-11-01.abstract: A week after the deadliest day of violence in Baghdad in more than two years, smaller bomb attacks around the country killed at least 12 and wounded more than 50.

2009-11-02.title: Out of Race, Karzai Rival Is Harsh Critic of Election
2009-11-02.abstract: Abdullah Abdullah announced that he would not participate in the Afghan runoff election, effectively handing a new term to President Hamid Karzai.

2009-11-02.title: Europe Still Likes Obama, but Doubts Creep In
2009-11-02.abstract: Many Europeans argue that President Obama has not broken enough with Bush administration policies, while some Americans say the Europeans are too passive and not providing much help.

2009-11-02.title: A Tweet Unleashes Vitriol on a User in Britain
2009-11-02.abstract: Attacks and counterattacks after a criticism of Stephen Fry on Twitter were examples of how Twitter reinforces the tendency of adults to behave like high school students.

2009-11-02.title: Israelis Arrest West Bank Settler in Attacks
2009-11-02.abstract: Jack Teitel, an immigrant from the United States, was charged with the murders of two Palestinians and two bombings that wounded a professor and a 15-year-old boy.

2009-11-02.title: Opposition in Iran Urges Continuing Challenge
2009-11-02.abstract: A day commemorating the 1979 takeover of the United States Embassy could turn into a showdown between protesters and the police.

2009-11-03.title: Times Responds on Reporter’s Kidnapping
2009-11-03.abstract: Several Web sites repeated Monday erroneous allegations that The New York Times had paid a ransom for the release of its reporter David Rohde, held by the Taliban for seven months.

2009-11-03.title: Where the Swearing Is All About the Context
2009-11-03.abstract: Mexicans, despite their reputation for formality, curse like sailors, a recent survey found. But exactly what is considered a bad word can require some interpretation.

2009-11-03.title: Iran: Reformist Newspaper Is Closed
2009-11-03.abstract: The authorities shut down Sarmayeh newspaper on charges of “repeated violations of the press law,” the official news agency IRNA reported.

2009-11-03.title: Karadzic Promises to Attend War Crimes Trial Tuesday
2009-11-03.abstract: Radovan Karadzic, the former Bosnian Serb leader, is defending himself but previously advised the tribunal that he was not ready for the trial.

2009-11-03.title: Scandal at Tower of London Over Treatment of Female Beefeater
2009-11-03.abstract: Three male guards are under investigation, suspected of bullying the first woman named to their ranks.

2009-11-04.title: 3 Yemeni Officials, Along With Guards, Killed in Ambush
2009-11-04.abstract: The ambush was the deadliest attack on government officials in years in Yemen, where Al Qaeda has declared a goal of overthrowing the Yemeni state.

2009-11-04.title: Groups Press U.S. and China on Carbon
2009-11-04.abstract: Two research organizations want the two countries to put more money into developing use of carbon capture technology.

2009-11-04.title: Africa’s Diamond Trade Under Scrutiny
2009-11-04.abstract: The viability of the Kimberley Process, the international effort to halt the trading of so-called blood diamonds, is on the line this week and the test case is Zimbabwe.

2009-11-04.title: An Obama Relative Living in China Tells of His Own Journey of Self-Discovery
2009-11-04.abstract: Mark Ndesandjo, who shares a father with President Obama and has kept a low profile during his half brother’s political ascent, has written an autobiographical novel.

2009-11-04.title: Chinese Trial Reveals Vast Web of Corruption
2009-11-04.abstract: A mass public trial has exposed the relationships between gangsters, police officers and sticky-fingered bureaucrats.

2009-11-05.title: Australia Puts Its Refugee Problem on a Remote Island, Behind Razor Wire
2009-11-05.abstract: The new Christmas Island refugee detention center has come to symbolize what many call one of Australia’s defining fears: the arrival of boat people from Asia .

2009-11-05.title: A City of Mixed Emotions Observes Calvin’s 500th
2009-11-05.abstract: In the Swiss city that is associated with the religious reformer John Calvin, some are seeking to update — even soften — his image on the anniversary of his birth.

2009-11-05.title: Pentagon Expected to Request More War Funding
2009-11-05.abstract: Adm. Mike Mullen, the chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, said he anticipated the need for more money for the wars in Iraq and Afghanistan in the coming year.

2009-11-05.title: Saudi Arabia: Attackers Kill Border Guard
2009-11-05.abstract: The attack appeared to be the first on Saudi Arabia by the Houthi rebels, who have fought intermittently against the Yemeni government for more than five years, analysts said.

2009-11-05.title: Britain: Conservatives Back Off Treaty Vote
2009-11-05.abstract: David Cameron announced Wednesday that he would not hold a referendum on the Lisbon Treaty if he became prime minister.

2009-11-06.title: Deadly Gas Flows Add to a Lake’s List of Perils
2009-11-06.abstract: The city of Goma and the surrounding area of eastern Congo hold many dangers, including rebellions, famine and a more mysterious threat: methane and carbon dioxide beneath Lake Kivu’s surface and along its shores.

2009-11-06.title: To Merkel, a Night in ’89 Was Just the First Step on a Long Path for 2 Germanys
2009-11-06.abstract: As the 20th anniversary of the fall of the Berlin Wall approaches, Chancellor Angela Merkel of Germany discussed the event and the long path Germans have taken since then to bring the two sides closer together.

2009-11-06.title: Tsvangirai Calls Off Cabinet-Meetings Boycott
2009-11-06.abstract: Prime Minister Morgan Tsvangirai of Zimbabwe said that his party had called off its boycott of cabinet meetings, but it was unclear what Mr. Tsvangirai received in return for backing down.

2009-11-06.title: Despite Deal, No Progress on Standoff in Honduras
2009-11-06.abstract: The ousted leader, Manuel Zelaya, is no closer to resuming his presidency, and the de facto president, Roberto Micheletti, is still running the country.

2009-11-06.title: Reviews Raise Doubt on Training of Afghan Forces
2009-11-06.abstract: The U.S. program is struggling to help largely illiterate and often corrupt Afghan forces, documents suggest.

2009-11-07.title: Prospect of More U.S. Troops Worries Afghan Public
2009-11-07.abstract: Eight years of war have left people in Afghanistan exhausted, impatient and increasingly unsure that the Taliban can be defeated.

2009-11-07.title: Ivory Coast: Judge Rejects Request in Toxic Waste Case
2009-11-07.abstract: About $45 million intended to compensate 30,000 victims of a toxic waste dumping scandal in Ivory Coast will not be diverted to a self-appointed community representative, a judge in Abidjan ruled Friday.

2009-11-07.title: Taiwan: Swine Flu Vaccination Program Begins
2009-11-07.abstract: The Taiwanese government announced that by starting vaccinations against the H1N1 virus this week, it had begun the largest immunization program in Taiwan’s history.

2009-11-07.title: Israel: Ministry Assails U.N. Vote on War Conduct
2009-11-07.abstract: Israel on Friday rejected a United Nations General Assembly vote endorsing a report accusing it of war crimes in Gaza last winter, saying that the resolution did not have “the support of the moral majority.”

2009-11-07.title: Same Booms, Different Terrain
2009-11-07.abstract: While it is sometimes useful to note the similarities in the Iraq and Afghanistan wars, in this case it is useful — it helps in your understanding — to note a very large difference.

2009-11-08.title: Corrections
2009-11-08.abstract: An article last Sunday about a deadly attack in October at a United Nations guesthouse in Kabul, Afghanistan, misstated the number of Afghan suspects arrested by that day. It was six, not eight.

2009-11-08.title: From the Arab Press: Coverage of the Fort Hood Killings
2009-11-08.abstract: Coverage in the Arab press of the mass killings at Fort Hood.

2009-11-08.title: All Afghan War Options by Obama Aides Said to Call for More Troops
2009-11-08.abstract: The president has apparently rejected the most extreme choices being discussed on troop levels in the eight-year-old war.

2009-11-08.title: China Pledges $10 Billion to Africa
2009-11-08.abstract: China offered African governments a multibillion-dollar package of financial and technical assistance.

2009-11-08.title: Pakistan Blast Kills Anti-Taliban Mayor
2009-11-08.abstract: A mayor who publicly opposed the Taliban was killed in a suicide bombing in a cattle market near the northern city of Peshawar.

2009-11-09.title: Saudis Claim to Beat Back Yemeni Rebels in Border Area
2009-11-09.abstract: Houthi rebels dismissed Saudi Arabia’s claim, saying Saudi troops had “collapsed” and retreated from the area.

2009-11-09.title: F1 Season Review, and a Mosley Coincidence
An Oasis Is No Match for Bulldozers and Bureaucrats
2009-11-09.abstract: The Soviet government’s land monopoly may have ended some two decades ago, but the ability of the authorities to give and take away territory has not, real estate experts say.

2009-11-09.title: The Legacy of 1989 Is Still Up for Debate
2009-11-09.abstract: Europe was transformed in 1989, but the events of that year are seen differently in Europe, the U.S. and Russia.

2009-11-09.title: Iran Said to Ignore Effort to Salvage Nuclear Deal
2009-11-09.abstract: The Obama administration told Iran in back-channel messages that it would allow it to send its enriched uranium to several nations for safekeeping.

2009-11-10.title: A Weaker Storm Devastated El Salvador
2009-11-10.abstract: Forecasters say a relatively small low-pressure cell, not Hurricane Ida, brought enormous quantities of rain and destruction.

2009-11-10.title: Women Ascend to Iraq’s Elite Police Officer Corps
2009-11-10.abstract: Fifty women joined 1,050 male classmates in what U.S. officials called a step forward for Iraq.

2009-11-10.title: Tensions in an Iranian Exile Camp in Iraq
2009-11-10.abstract: Iraq is losing patience with the Iranian group known as the People’s Mujahedeen, and a standoff has been in place since a raid on Camp Ashraf, where the group is based.

2009-11-10.title: For Taliban Fighters, a Fading Memorial
2009-11-10.abstract: A remote cemetery offers a history of the era when young jihadists traveled to Afghanistan to fight.

2009-11-10.title: Zimbabwe: Party Leader’s Trial Begins
2009-11-10.abstract: The trial of Roy Bennett, a leader in the Movement for Democratic Change, started amid signs that President Robert Mugabe was continuing to crack down on independent groups.

2009-11-11.title: U.S. Tries to Salvage Honduras Accord
2009-11-11.abstract: Deputy Assistant Secretary of State Craig Kelly went to Honduras to meet with the ousted president and the head of the de facto government.

2009-11-11.title: Influence of Egypt and Saudi Arabia Fades
2009-11-11.abstract: America’s closest Arab allies are increasingly viewed in the region as diminished actors, political experts say.

2009-11-11.title: Blackouts Plague Energy-Rich Venezuela
2009-11-11.abstract: With his nation plagued by power failures and water shortages, President Hugo Chávez has turned to lecturing about overconsumption.

2009-11-11.title: France: Court Rules a Lesbian Can Adopt
2009-11-11.abstract: The government of President Nicolas Sarkozy responded to the court ruling by declaring its continued opposition to adoption by gay couples.

2009-11-11.title: 3 Obama Advisers Favor More Troops for Afghanistan
2009-11-11.abstract: The president’s national security team is coalescing around a proposal to send 30,000 or more additional troops to Afghanistan, officials say.

2009-11-12.title: Japan Cools to America as It Prepares for Obama Visit
2009-11-12.abstract: President Obama’s visit to Tokyo on Friday will come at a time when relations with Japan are strained.

2009-11-12.title: Okinawans Grow Impatient With Dashed Hopes on U.S. Base
2009-11-12.abstract: The issue of the American air base on Okinawa has taken on heightened significance and is perhaps the most prominent issue President Obama will face during his visit.

2009-11-12.title: North Korea Warns South After Naval Clash
2009-11-12.abstract: The United States said a naval skirmish would not deter it from sending an envoy to North Korea.

2009-11-12.title: Charges Prompt Iraqis to Look Into Blackwater
2009-11-12.abstract: The Iraqi interior minister ordered an investigation into whether top Blackwater officials approved of bribes to Iraqi government officials after fatal shootings in 2007.

2009-11-12.title: Push to Build Mosques Is Met With Resistance
2009-11-12.abstract: The difficulties reflect the tortuous path Denmark has taken in dealing with its immigrants, most of whom are Muslim.

2009-11-13.title: W.H.O. Rushes Drugs to Nations Hit by Swine Flu
2009-11-13.abstract: Emergency supplies of antiviral drugs are being sent to Eastern Europe and Central Asia, where hospitals report they are being overwhelmed by patients with swine flu.

2009-11-13.title: White House Sides With C.I.A. in Turf Battle
2009-11-13.abstract: A dispute over the power to pick the top spy in each country overseas showed limits of the intelligence bureaucracy overhaul.

2009-11-13.title: Among Obama Aides, Debate Intensifies on Troop Levels
2009-11-13.abstract: The disclosure that the U.S. ambassador to Afghanistan has expressed opposition to deploying more troops lays bare a fierce debate within the Obama administration.

2009-11-13.title: Gates Condemns Leaks on U.S. Afghan Policy and Ft. Hood
2009-11-13.abstract: The defense secretary said disclosing unauthorized information would be a career-ender in his department and said: “Everybody ought to just shut up.”

2009-11-13.title: Ill Will Grows in a Former Colonial Region
2009-11-13.abstract: As democracy slips in country after country, France often quietly sides with the once-and-future autocrats.

2009-11-14.title: Saving Sea Turtles, One Nest at a Time
2009-11-14.abstract: Global warming and coastal development are decimating Pacific sea turtle populations. In Costa Rica, a group of one-time poachers is giving baby sea turtles a chance at survival.

2009-11-14.title: Outsourcing Jobs Arrive in Rural Areas
2009-11-14.abstract: A handful of entrepreneurs are bringing outsourcing jobs to rural areas in India that have been largely cut off from its economic rise.

2009-11-14.title: Fragile Care Worsened Swine Flu in Ukraine
2009-11-14.abstract: A frenzy for care in Ukraine has drawn attention from experts bracing for the epidemic to hit Europe, and especially the flimsy health care systems of countries of the former Soviet Union.

2009-11-14.title: China Focuses on Territorial Issues as It Equates Tibet to U.S. Civil War South
2009-11-14.abstract: While much attention during President Obama’s visit will be focused on trade and climate change, questions of sovereignty and territory remain an obsession of Chinese foreign policy.

2009-11-14.title: An Arms Dealer Returns, Now Selling an Image
2009-11-14.abstract: Adnan M. Khashoggi, the arms dealer involved in Iran-Contra and other major scandals, is trying to make a comeback, now selling his connections.

2009-11-15.title: In China, Obama to Press for Tough Stance on Iran
2009-11-15.abstract: President Obama will push China’s leaders to get on board for possible tough new sanctions against Iran.

2009-11-15.title: Czechs’ Velvet Revolution Paved by Plastic People
2009-11-15.abstract: A band’s refusal to cut its hair, its willingness to brave prison cells and its talent for tapping into a generation’s collective despair helped change the direction of a nation.

2009-11-16.title: U.S. Readies New Facility for Afghan Detainees
2009-11-16.abstract: The new complex is part of a broader effort to alter America’s detention image, which has been badly tarnished by reports of abuse.

2009-11-16.title: Antismoking Fight Proves a Pyramid-Size Task
2009-11-16.abstract: Water pipes and cigarettes are still found everywhere, despite laws and appeals to health concerns, but antismoking forces are making another try.

2009-11-16.title: Obama Hobbled in Fight Against Global Warming
2009-11-16.abstract: President Obama’s ambitions are limited by a Congress that is unwilling to move as far or as fast as he would like.

2009-11-16.title: In Nicaragua, Opposition Sees an End Run
2009-11-16.abstract: President Daniel Ortega has been among the most calculated in the region in stymieing opposition to his bid to maintain power.

2009-11-16.title: Yemen Finds Dreamland of Architecture
2009-11-16.abstract: Traditions in Yemen have stayed largely alive because of deep poverty and long isolation, making the capital’s Old City one of the world’s architectural gems.

2009-11-17.title: After Quake in Italy, Seeking Greener Pastures
2009-11-17.abstract: An annual migration of sheep in the Abruzzo region assumed greater resonance as the flock passed through areas hit by the April 6 quake.

2009-11-17.abstract: The women who answered a modeling agency’s ad were surprised to end up being offered the Koran and urged to convert to Islam by Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi.

2009-11-17.abstract: A meeting in Rome between sixty world leaders underscored the split between rich and poor countries on the issue of fighting hunger.

2009-11-17.title: Zambia Judge Clears Editor in Morals Case Over Photos
2009-11-17.abstract: The journalist had distributed pictures of a woman giving birth outside a hospital that had turned her away during a strike by health workers.

2009-11-17.title: Britain: Police Arrest Five Men in Antiterrorism Operation
2009-11-17.abstract: The arrests were in connection with what police described as “the incitement of terrorism overseas.”

2009-11-18.title: Ripe for the Plucking, but Fewer Dare to Try
2009-11-18.abstract: A scarcity of people willing to do the risky work of coconut plucking threatens India’s coconut industry and illustrates the loosening of caste bonds.

2009-11-18.title: Pierre Harmel, Belgian Who Helped Reshape NATO, Dies at 98
2009-11-18.abstract: Mr. Harmel was a former prime minister but had his greatest impact as the foreign minister when he was deeply involved in the future of the Atlantic alliance.

2009-11-18.title: Nationalists Suspected in Russian Activist’s Death
2009-11-18.abstract: A young antiracist campaigner, identified by Russian news agencies as Ivan Khutorskoi, was shot in the head in front of his apartment building.

2009-11-18.title: Coconut Pluckers in Short Supply
2009-11-18.abstract: Kerala, India, is in the grips of an acute labor shortage that threatens to undermine one of its most important industries.

2009-11-18.title: Pakistani Military Encounters Little Fight as Militants Flee
2009-11-18.abstract: The army has made gains after sweeping operations against militants, but lasting success may prove elusive.

2009-11-19.title: 2007 Memo Raised Concerns About Hasan
2009-11-19.abstract: Reports that Maj. Nidal Malik Hasan received a previous evaluation that might have prevented him from finding work outside the military.

2009-11-19.title: A Saudi Gamble to See if Seeds of Change Will Grow
2009-11-19.abstract: King Abdullah of Saudi Arabia has given a university $10 billion, hoping it will draw ideas and students.

2009-11-19.title: A Soccer Match in Sudan Includes Soldiers and Guns
2009-11-19.abstract: As Egypt and Algeria’s powerhouse soccer teams squared off for a chance to go to the World Cup, the host Sudanese were not playing games.

2009-11-19.abstract: Facing a vote Thursday to finally pick a slate of new and more powerful leaders, the E.U. is falling back on old-fashioned deal-making that critics say may produce lackluster choices.

2009-11-19.title: Mideast Peace Talks Hang in Balance Over Abbas
2009-11-19.abstract: The Israeli security establishment is alarmed over the possible departure of President Mahmoud Abbas, whom it considers a true moderate.

2009-11-20.title: China Helps the Powerful in Namibia
2009-11-20.abstract: Secret scholarships to study in China that were awarded to the offspring of top officials in Namibia have angered the public there.

2009-11-20.title: Industrialized Nations Unveil Plans to Rein in Emissions
2009-11-20.abstract: Prior to a climate change meeting scheduled for Copenhagen, industrialized countries, except the United States, are offering targets to curb greenhouse gases.

2009-11-20.title: Pakistani Politics Take on a Nationalist Tone
2009-11-20.abstract: The recent unpopularity of President Asif Ali Zardari and resentment of America follow a familiar script.

2009-11-20.title: Anglican Leader Defends Faith as Vatican Welcomes His Members
2009-11-20.abstract: In Rome, the archbishop of Canterbury called for clarity on the future of Catholic-Anglican dialogue.

2009-11-20.title: Clinton Seen as Obama’s Key Link to Afghan Leader
2009-11-20.abstract: The role thrusts Secretary of State Hillary Rodham Clinton into the thick of a critical international problem.

2009-11-21.title: New Leaders in Japan Seek to End Cozy Ties to Press Clubs
2009-11-21.abstract: The authorities are expanding access to journalists who don’t belong to cartel-like media groups that analysts say have produced a relatively spineless press.

2009-11-21.title: Canada: Court Orders Refugee Board to Consider ‘Don’t Ask, Don’t Tell’ in Army Deserter’s Case
2009-11-21.abstract: The Federal Court ruled Friday that a refugee board must consider the treatment of homosexuals by the United States military when examining the case of an Army deserter who is a lesbian.

2009-11-21.title: Proton Beams Are on Track at Collider
2009-11-21.abstract: The successful operation of the Large Hadron Collider marked the resumption of the world’s biggest and most expensive physics experiment.

2009-11-21.title: Corrections
2009-11-21.abstract: An article last Saturday about President Obama’s visit to Japan, where he announced creation of a group to study the contentious issue of the Marine base on Okinawa, misstated, in some editions, the year in which a Japanese 12-year-old was raped on Okinawa by three American servicemen. It was 1995, not 1996.

2009-11-21.title: Italian Prosecutors Ask for Life Sentence in Trial of U.S. Student
2009-11-21.abstract: Prosecutors asked an Italian court to hand down life sentences to an American student and her former boyfriend for their alleged roles in a fatal stabbing.

2009-11-22.title: 3 Suspected Militants Blow Themselves Up in Pakistani Kashmir
2009-11-22.abstract: The suspects were being pursued by the police after weapons were reported dumped along a road.

2009-11-22.title: Son Objects to Moving Camus’s Remains
2009-11-22.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy wants to transfer the remains of the writer Albert Camus to one of the most hallowed burial places in France, but the plan has run into opposition from the Nobel laureate’s son.

2009-11-22.title: Amnesty List Puts Pressure on Pakistan Leader
2009-11-22.abstract: In another blow to Pakistan’s president, authorities released the names of politicians who benefited from an amnesty decree that dismissed past alleged crimes.

2009-11-22.title: Iran’s Death Penalty Is Seen as a Political Tactic
2009-11-22.abstract: A flurry of executions has raised concern that the government is using judicially sanctioned killing to quell pockets of unrest around the nation.

2009-11-23.title: In Philippines Strife, Uprooting Is a Constant
2009-11-23.abstract: A long-running conflict between the government and Muslim separatists on the island of Mindanao has made displacement a regular part of refugees’ lives.

2009-11-23.title: Konstantin Feoktistov, a Soviet Spacecraft Engineer, Dies at 83
2009-11-23.abstract: Mr. Feoktistov, for whom a crater was named on the Moon, was a Soviet engineer and astronaut, and a prominent spacecraft designer.

2009-11-23.title: Analysis: Voting With Bombs
2009-11-23.abstract: Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki’s most potent opponents in Iraq are the extremists who have proven able to carry out spectacular attacks that undermine his core argument, that things are better.

2009-11-23.abstract: A primer on the military utility of some of the force options before President Obama to bolster the 68,000 American troops already in Afghanistan.

2009-11-23.abstract: Canada’s top military officer said that the Canadians had sometimes refrained from turning over prisoners to the Afghan government for fear they would be tortured.

2009-11-24.title: Obama Issues Sharp Rebuke of Mugabe
2009-11-24.abstract: In honoring Zimbabwe’s tenacious women protesters at the White House on Monday, President Obama bluntly referred to Zimbabwe’s president as a dictator.

2009-11-24.title: Russia: 8 Die in Blast at Weapons Depot
2009-11-24.abstract: The explosion in central Russia occurred while soldiers were disposing of unexploded munitions left over from an earlier explosion on Nov. 13, a Defense Ministry spokesman said.

2009-11-24.title: Iran Sentences Journalist to Jail Time
2009-11-24.abstract: Iran handed down a five-year jail term to Ahmad Zeidabadi, a prominent journalist and former student leader, an opposition Web site reported.

2009-11-24.title: Iran Expanding Effort to Stifle the Opposition
2009-11-24.abstract: The government is launching an ambitious effort to discredit its opponents and re-educate the country’s youth.

2009-11-24.abstract: President Obama is facing criticism from Democrats over the war’s cost, the size of the U.S. commitment and the reliability of allies.

2009-11-25.title: A Bed Where Comfort Is Not the Point
2009-11-25.abstract: The latest health fad to capture the interest of many Swedes is a modern-day nail bed made with small plastic disks and sharp little spikes.

2009-11-25.title: India’s Guard Is Up, but Weaknesses Remain
2009-11-25.abstract: A year after the deadly assault in Mumbai that killed 163 people, the city has made limited security improvements.

2009-11-25.title: Obama Writes to Brazil’s Leader About Iran
2009-11-25.abstract: President Obama reiterated the American position on Iran’s nuclear program a day before Iran’s president, Mahmoud Ahmadinejad, visited Brazil.

2009-11-25.title: Afghan Strategy Will Contain Messages to Several Audiences
2009-11-25.abstract: As President Obama rolls out his plan, he will be adjusting his approach for voters, allies and enemies.

2009-11-25.title: Uzbekistan: Opposition Leader Sent to U.S. After Early Release From Prison
2009-11-25.abstract: Sanjar Umarov, who was imprisoned on charges that his supporters said were fabricated, was granted amnesty and released from a labor camp on Nov. 7.

2009-11-26.title: Mumbai Still Vulnerable on Anniversary of Attacks
2009-11-26.abstract: A year after the deadly assault that killed 163 people, Mumbai is a more cautious and safer place, but remains vulnerable.

2009-11-26.abstract: President Obama will talk about the troop increase and bringing troops home, as he walks a fine line between demonstrating resolve and reassuring Americans that the commitment to Afghanistan is not open-ended.

2009-11-26.abstract: NATO allies appear willing to send fewer than half that number of additional troops to Afghanistan as part of President Obama’s strategy for the region, officials said.

2009-11-26.title: Soul-Searching in Turkey After a Gay Man Is Killed
2009-11-26.abstract: Ahmet Yildiz was shot last year outside his apartment in Istanbul, in what may have been the first “honor killing” of a gay man to gain public attention.

2009-11-26.title: In Welcoming Iranian President, Chavez Blasts Israel
2009-11-26.abstract: President Hugo Chávez of Venezuela described Israel as “the murderous arm of the Yankee empire.”

2009-11-27.title: Report Says Irish Bishops and Police Hid Abuse
2009-11-27.abstract: The church and police colluded to hide decades of child abuse by priests in Dublin, an investigation concluded.

2009-11-27.title: A Remote Island Seeks a Boom Without a Bust
2009-11-27.abstract: Residents are hoping a new immigration detention center can revive the remote Australian island’s economy.

2009-11-27.title: Iranian Militiamen Try to Cow Opposition Figure
2009-11-27.abstract: A demonstration by hard-liners came as another opposition leader’s Nobel Prize was confiscated.

2009-11-27.title: Ali Kordan, Iranian Official Fired Over Fake Degree, Dies at 51
2009-11-27.abstract: Mr. Kordan was dismissed by Iran’s Parliament after it became clear that he had lied about an honorary doctor of law degree he said he had received from Oxford.

2009-11-27.title: Taliban Open Northern Front in Afghanistan
2009-11-27.abstract: Militants have had a resurgence in Kunduz, a province that U.S. officials didn’t think they had to worry about.

2009-11-28.title: Montreal Grand Prix Is Back On for 2010
2009-11-28.abstract: After a one-year absence from North America, Formula One racing will return to Montreal in 2010, with a Grand Prix race scheduled for June 13 on the site of the Expo 67 world’s fair.

2009-11-28.title: John Burns Q. and A. on Obama’s Options in Afghanistan
2009-11-28.abstract: John Burns answers readers’ questions on some possible force options available to President Obama as he considers bolstering the 68,000 American troops already in Afghanistan.

2009-11-28.title: Experts Say Swine Flu Mutations Do Not Warrant New Alarm
2009-11-28.abstract: Scientists with the World Health Organization say that new instances of resistance to Tamiflu have been rare so far.

2009-11-28.title: China Jails Environmentalist Wanted in U.S.
2009-11-28.abstract: An American man who spent four years hiding in a backpacker haven to evade charges of ecoterrorism was convicted of manufacturing drugs.

2009-11-28.title: China’s Impolitic Artist, Still Waiting to Be Silenced
2009-11-28.abstract: Ai Weiwei is perhaps China’s most famous living artist and its most vociferous domestic critic, titles of a sort the committed iconoclast disdains.

2009-11-29.title: Villagers Rushed to Help in Frigid Russian Crash
2009-11-29.abstract: The Russian train bombing took place in an area so remote that people from nearby towns got to the site hours before rescue workers.

2009-11-29.title: Pakistan’s Leader Cedes Nuclear Office
2009-11-29.abstract: President Asif Ali Zardari handed over his position in the nuclear command structure to his prime minister, in a sudden maneuver widely seen as a fresh sign of turmoil.

2009-11-29.title: Rwanda Joins the Commonwealth
2009-11-29.abstract: The republic of Rwanda was admitted to the Commonwealth on Saturday, becoming the 54th nation to join the post-colonial group.

2009-11-29.title: Corrections
2009-11-29.abstract: An article last Sunday about Grand Ayatollah Hossein Ali Montazeri of Iran, who has emerged as the spiritual leader of the political opposition, misstated, in some editions, the surname of an Iranian scholar who said Ayatollah Montazeri’s criticisms were especially hurtful to the government because they attacked its assertion of Islamic legitimacy. The scholar, a former seminary student who is now senior fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy, is Mehdi Khalaji, not Khaliji.

2009-11-29.title: Traumatized Russians View Their Dead After Luxury Train Bombing
2009-11-29.abstract: Across the country, Russians prayed and held moments of silence for the victims of a homemade bomb planted on train tracks.

2009-11-30.title: A Museum Hails Caesar, Even if Some Antiquarians Don’t Agree
2009-11-30.abstract: A marble bust dredged up from the Rhône River is thought to be the only known surviving statue of Julius Caesar carved during his lifetime, though some question its authenticity.

2009-11-30.title: Tree Harvester Offers to Save Indonesian Forest
2009-11-30.abstract: A paper company proposes to use a ring of tree plantations to help stop leaks of greenhouse gases from a peat bog.

2009-11-30.title: Britain Presses Pakistan and Afghanistan on Militants
2009-11-30.abstract: Prime Minister Gordon Brown demanded that Afghanistan and Pakistan match plans for increased allied troop levels in Afghanistan by taking tough actions of their own.

2009-11-30.title: Obama’s Speech on Afghanistan to Envision Exit
2009-11-30.abstract: President Obama plans to lay out a time frame for U.S. involvement in the Afghan war when he announces his decision this week to send more forces, officials said.

2009-11-30.title: Elections in Honduras
2009-11-30.abstract: Hondurans went to the polls in a presidential election that many voters hoped would allow the country to emerge from the political crisis caused by last summerâ€™s coup.
